alertitem.alert: CSP: Failure to Define Directive with No Fallback
alertitem.name: CSP: Failure to Define Directive with No Fallback
alertitem.desc: The Content Security Policy fails to define one of the directives that has no fallback. Missing/excluding them is the same as allowing anything.

instance.evidence: default-src 'none'; script-src 'self'; connect-src 'self'; child-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; font-src 'self' data:; style-src 'self'
instance.otherinfo: The directive(s): frame-ancestors, form-action is/are among the directives that do not fallback to default-src.

instance.evidence: default-src 'none'; script-src 'self'; connect-src 'self'; child-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; font-src 'self' data:; style-src 'self'
instance.otherinfo: The directive(s): frame-ancestors, form-action is/are among the directives that do not fallback to default-src.

instance.evidence: default-src 'none'; script-src 'self'; connect-src 'self'; child-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; font-src 'self' data:; style-src 'self'
instance.otherinfo: The directive(s): frame-ancestors, form-action is/are among the directives that do not fallback to default-src.

instance.evidence: default-src 'none'; script-src 'self'; connect-src 'self'; child-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; font-src 'self' data:; style-src 'self'
instance.otherinfo: The directive(s): frame-ancestors, form-action is/are among the directives that do not fallback to default-src.

instance.evidence: default-src 'none'; script-src 'self'; connect-src 'self'; child-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; font-src 'self' data:; style-src 'self'
instance.otherinfo: The directive(s): frame-ancestors, form-action is/are among the directives that do not fallback to default-src.

alertitem.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is properly configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.
alertitem.otherinfo: The directive(s): frame-ancestors, form-action is/are among the directives that do not fallback to default-src.

alertitem.name: Authentication Request Identified
alertitem.desc: The given request has been identified as an authentication request. The 'Other Info' field contains a set of key=value lines which identify any relevant fields. If the request is in a context which has an Authentication Method set to "Auto-Detect" then this rule will change the authentication to match the request identified.

instance.otherinfo: userParam=username userValue=ZAP passwordParam=password referer=http://localhost:8081/UI/network/action/setHttpProxy/
userParam=username userValue=ZAP passwordParam=password referer=http://localhost:8081/UI/network/action/setSocksProxy/

alertitem.solution: This is an informational alert rather than a vulnerability and so there is nothing to fix.

alertitem.alert: Information Disclosure - Sensitive Information in URL
alertitem.name: Information Disclosure - Sensitive Information in URL
alertitem.desc: The request appeared to contain sensitive information leaked in the URL. This can violate PCI and most organizational compliance policies. You can configure the list of strings for this check to add or remove values specific to your environment.

instance.otherinfo: The URL contains potentially sensitive information. The following string was found via the pattern: user userName
The URL contains potentially sensitive information. The following string was found via the pattern: user userName
The URL contains potentially sensitive information. The following string was found via the pattern: token sessionToken
The URL contains potentially sensitive information. The following string was found via the pattern: token sessionToken
The URL contains potentially sensitive information. The following string was found via the pattern: session session

alertitem.solution: Do not pass sensitive information in URIs.
alertitem.otherinfo:
The URL contains potentially sensitive information. The following string was found via the pattern: user

userName

alertitem.name: User Agent Fuzzer
alertitem.desc: Check for differences in response based on fuzzed User Agent (eg. mobile sites, access as a Search Engine Crawler). Compares the response statuscode and the hashcode of the response body with the original response.

alertitem.alert: User Controllable HTML Element Attribute (Potential XSS)
alertitem.name: User Controllable HTML Element Attribute (Potential XSS)
alertitem.desc: This check looks at user-supplied input in query string parameters and POST data to identify where certain HTML attribute values might be controlled. This provides hot-spot detection for XSS (cross-site scripting) that will require further review by a security analyst to determine exploitability.

instance.otherinfo:
User-controlled HTML attribute values were found. Try injecting special characters to see if XSS might be possible. The page at the following URL:
